scope: Release APK selected-agent 30-minute idle request-rate and device power soak on a real Android Emulator using ADB UIAutomator instead of Flutter Driver.

mobile_app.head: f2acc00
mobile_app.commit: docs: record release idle avd smoke

source.head: 7e436f7e
source.commit: fix: order mobile native transcript pages by record time

release_apk.build_warning: file_picker and mobile_scanner still use plugin-applied Kotlin Gradle Plugin; Flutter reports this as a future deprecation warning.

ui_smoke.automation: ADB UIAutomator XML plus adb input tap; no Flutter Driver.

acceptance_notes: The run started two disposable real /home/bfly/yunwei/test_ccb2 projects and a loopback-only server-wide gateway.
The release APK was seeded with the paired profile, installed, and launched without Flutter Driver.
The release app opened test_ccb2_alpha/mobile_probe from the real server-wide project list before the idle audit window.
A request-counting proxy observed zero gateway requests during the 1800-second untouched selected-agent idle window.
Device metrics showed only 485 KB PSS growth, no app wake locks, no FATAL/ANR/OOM logcat marker, and no skipped-frame logcat marker.
residual_risks: This closes release 30-minute selected-agent idle request-rate and power soak.
Release long-history frame/memory pressure, release file performance, and recovery pressure remain separate work.
